title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox won 3-0 against the Reds, with Garrett Crochet and the bullpen pitching a shutout.
New ABS Challenge System allowed Roman Anthony's disputed strike to be overturned, extending an inning.
Anthony's challenge led to key RBIs from Trevor Story and Jarren Duran in the ninth inning.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer, 23, debuted as a pinch-hitter for the Red Sox during their Opening Day game against the Reds.
Mayer contributed significantly with a 2-for-2 performance, scoring two runs in Boston's 3-0 victory.
Despite initial benching due to offensive struggles, Mayer's performance strengthened his alongside potential with the team.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog cut his leg on a solar light while owners were on vacation in New Hampshire.
The dog's sitter sought help from Campton-Thornton Fire Department as bleeding worsened en route to a vet.
Firefighters applied a tourniquet, and Ziggy later underwent successful surgery, averting life-threatening blood loss.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy owner faces excessive oil consumption and is advised to replace the short block for about $4,000.
Subaru engines may have oil control piston ring issues, potentially resolved with engine flushes and multiple oil changes.
Alternative oil consumption solutions include checking the PCV system, while a new engine may ultimately resolve the problem.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner in Nantucket sells fifth $1 million lottery ticket since March 2024, according to state lottery commission.
The diner received a $20,000 bonus for selling a second $2 million lottery ticket this March, officials said.
The latest win continues a streak starting with a $4 Million Bonus Bucks ticket prize in March 2024.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, a former Boston school bus driver, was arraigned for involuntary manslaughter and vehicle homicide after a crash killed a child.
Prosecutors allege Charles failed to perform a safety inspection and drove recklessly during the April 28, 2025, Hyde Park incident.
Months after the crash, Charles was involved in a separate hit-and-run in Brockton, charged with leaving the scene and driving with a suspended license.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet led the Red Sox to a 3-0 win with six strong innings and eight strikeouts.
Marcelo Mayer's crucial double and single contributed to key runs, ensuring a Red Sox victory.
Roman Anthony went 3 for 4 at the plate, demonstrating strong hitting and strategic play decisions.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, missing since October, was found dead in Crystal Lake, Gardner nearly five months after escaping from Heywood Hospital.
Mercury, 57, escaped the hospital in distress, wearing only hospital clothes and no shoes, officials reported.
Gardner police used drones to locate Mercury's body in the lake; the cause of death remains under investigation.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after recovering from a femur tumor surgery.
Southern was told to avoid running post-surgery but has been cleared for activity after 10 years of recovery.
She is raising funds for Mass General Hospital, crediting it for her recovery and restored active lifestyle.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Jennifer Leah Goetz, a Cambridge pediatric doctor, is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General Hospital patients.
Goetz uses running to manage depression and started running to help raise funds for children's cancer treatment at MGH.
She trained in pediatrics and child psychiatry, working closely with children battling cancer during her time at MGH.
